items.title: Trump says Iran's handling of Strait of Hormuz is 'not the agreement we have'
items.summary: The US president accused Iran of "doing a very poor job" with the waterway as Israel and Lebanon to begin peace talks.

items.title: Ceasefire or no ceasefire, the Middle East's reshuffling is not yet done
items.summary: Both sides have reason to end the war but share no common ground.

items.title: Melania Trump denies ties to Jeffrey Epstein and urges hearing for survivors
items.summary: She called rumours that Epstein introduced her to Donald Trump "mean-spirited attempts to defame my reputation".

items.title: UK says Russia ran submarine operation over cables and pipelines
items.summary: Defence Secretary John Healey says there is "no evidence" of any damage to UK infrastructure in the Atlantic.

items.title: White House staff told not to place bets on prediction markets
items.summary: The gambling platforms have grown in popularity, with some users making wagers on conflicts.

items.title: Russia and Ukraine agree to truce for Orthodox Easter
items.summary: Vladimir Putin announced the truce, which will last from Saturday afternoon on 11 April through Easter Sunday.

items.title: Al Jazeera condemns killing of journalist in Israeli strike in Gaza
items.summary: The Israeli military claims Mohammed Wishah was a "Hamas terrorist" - which the Qatar-based network has previously denied.

items.title: US has let in 4,499 refugees since October - all but three were South African
items.summary: Trump has given priority to Afrikaners, a white minority group he says is being persecuted. South Africa has objected to his characterisation.

items.title: Artemis crew returning to Earth with 'all the good stuff' from Moon discoveries
items.summary: The four astronauts said they were returning to Earth with "so many more pictures, so many more stories".

items.title: Hip-hop pioneer, Afrika Bambaataa,  dies aged 68
items.summary: The Bronx born rapper's global hit Planet Rock is credited with shaping hip-hop in the 1980s.

items.title: India proposes new rules to regulate news and political posts on social media
items.summary: The changes will give government more power over news-related posts shared by podcasters and influencers.

items.title: Trump criticises Nato as alliance chief describes meeting as 'very frank'
items.summary: The US president says Nato "wasn't there when we needed them" during the Iran war.

items.title: French far-right leader romantically linked to Italian princess
items.summary: Presidential hopeful Jordan Bardella was pictured on a Corsican beach with Princess Maria Carolina of Bourbon-Two Sicilies.

items.title: Amazon to end support for older Kindles, prompting user outcry
items.summary: The move means owners of Kindles released before 2013 will be unable to download new e-books.

items.title: Spreading Islamist insurgency dominates Benin's presidential campaign
items.summary: Cross-border raids from militant Islamists are increasingly threatening the West African state's stability.

items.title: 'Endless fears': Even if fighting stops, the damage to Iran's children will endure
items.summary: The BBC has been able to obtain testimony from parents and those trying to help children deal with the distress that comes with war.

items.title: Trapped miner rescued from flooded Mexican tunnel after 14 days
items.summary: The 42-year-old gold miner was standing in waist-high water when rescuers found him in a flooded tunnel.

items.title: He's Australia's most decorated soldier. Now he's at the centre of a historic war crimes case
items.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith's case is not only unprecedented for Australia but "extraordinary" for the globe too, scholars say.

items.title: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
items.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

items.title: Pioneering wildlife cameraman Doug Allan dies after falling ill in Nepal
items.summary: The cameraman and photographer won eight Emmy Awards for his work on acclaimed series like Blue Planet alongside Sir David Attenborough.

items.title: Best-selling The Housemaid author Freida McFadden reveals true identity
items.summary: One of the biggest mysteries in publishing is solved, as The Housemaid writer reveals her real name.

items.title: Petrol and diesel prices rise again as concerns grow over ceasefire
items.summary: Motoring groups have warned drivers not to expect a significant drop in costs soon.

items.title: Ships remain cautious approaching Strait of Hormuz amid fragile ceasefire
items.summary: Only a few vessels have crossed the strait since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to BBC Verify analysis.

items.title: 'Ketamine Queen' sentenced to 15 years in Matthew Perry overdose death
items.summary: Jasveen Sangha was found guilty of selling drugs that killed Friends actor Matthew Perry who had struggled with addiction for years.

items.title: Prosecutors seek Tiger Woods' prescription drug records after Florida arrest
items.summary: A legal order seeking the golfer's medications - including dosage and warnings about driving on pill bottles - will be issued later this month, court record shows.

items.title: Germany suspends military approval for long stays abroad for men under 45
items.summary: The requirement to obtain permission had gone largely unnoticed until a German newspaper reported on it.

items.title: Hot in the city: Energy crisis tests Singapore's air-con addiction
items.summary: The rise in energy prices has hit Asia particularly hard as many nations are heavily reliant on Gulf oil.

items.title: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
items.summary: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

items.title: Has US achieved its war objectives in Iran?
items.summary: Key US objectives at the start of the war were to stop Iran getting a nuclear weapon and degrading its arsenal.

items.title: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
items.summary: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

items.title: The US refinery now processing Venezuelan oil
items.summary: Chevron is now importing 250,000 barrels of crude per day from Venezuela.

items.title: Indian migrant workers hit by cooking gas shortages are leaving cities
items.summary: Migrant workers say they are struggling to access cooking gas cylinders as the Iran war squeezes supplies.

items.title: Has Artemis II shown we can land on the Moon again?
items.summary: The Artemis II mission has been near flawless to date, but has the test flight shown Nasa is ready to send humans to the lunar surface?

items.title: Melania Trump: 'I never had a relationship with Epstein'
items.summary: Watch the first lady's full statement from the White House as she addresses her links to the late sex offender.

items.title: BBC at the site of Israeli air strikes in Beirut
items.summary: At least 182 people were killed and 890 others wounded, according to the Lebanese health ministry.

items.title: Watch: BBC asks Artemis II crew a question in space
items.summary: The BBC’s Science Editor Rebecca Morelle has spoken to the Artemis II crew ahead of their likely splashdown on Saturday.

items.title: Watch: Pope Leo XIV spins basketball on his finger, with help from Harlem Globetrotters
items.summary: US exhibition squad, the Harlem Globetrotters, met the pontiff in the Vatican, helping him to spin a basketball on the tip of his finger.
